model_narrative: We plan production over a sequence of periods to meet a known demand in each period. For each period we decide how much to produce, whether to set up production that period, and how much inventory to carry over to the next period. Setting up production in a period incurs a fixed setup cost, producing units incurs a per-unit production cost, and carrying inventory incurs a per-unit holding cost each period. The objective is to minimize the total cost over the whole horizon.

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the planning periods in chronological order; the first member is the opening period and each later member follows the one before it, and the last member is the final period of the horizon

params.name: setupcost
params.doc: the fixed cost incurred in any period in which production is set up, in dollars per period; a scalar

params.name: prodcost
params.doc: the variable cost of producing one unit, in dollars per unit; a scalar

params.name: invcost
params.doc: the base cost of holding one unit in inventory for one period, in dollars per unit per period; a scalar

params.name: stockini
params.doc: the inventory on hand at the very start of the horizon, before the first period, in units; a scalar

params.name: demand
params.doc: the demand that must be met in each period, in units

params.name: bigM
params.doc: a per-period upper bound on how much can be produced in that period; used as the large constant that links production to the setup decision, in units

params.name: invcoef
params.doc: the per-period inventory holding cost coefficient applied to ending inventory; equal to the base holding cost in every period except the final period of the horizon, where it is half the base holding cost, in dollars per unit per period

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the inventory held at the end of each period, in units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity produced in each period, in units

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the setup indicator for each period; equals 1 if production is set up in that period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole horizon, in dollars; a scalar

description: Production can only happen in a period when that period has been set up for production. For each period the amount produced must not exceed that period's production upper bound when the period is set up, and must be zero when the period is not set up.

vars.name: x

vars.name: y

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole horizon, in dollars; a scalar

description: Inventory must be conserved from one period to the next. For each period, the stock carried in from the previous period together with what is produced that period must cover that period's demand and leave the ending inventory for that period. In the very first period there is no previous period, so the starting inventory on hand at the beginning of the horizon plays that role instead.
expected_pyomo:
def balance_rule(model, t):
    t_list = list(model.t)
    is_first = (t_list.index(t) < 1)
    prev = 0 if is_first else model.s[model.t.prev(t)]
    ini = model.stockini if is_first else 0
    return ini + prev + model.x[t] == model.demand[t] + model.s[t]
model.balance = Constraint(model.t, rule=balance_rule)

The objective is to minimize the total cost over the whole horizon.

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole horizon, in dollars; a scalar

description: The total cost gathers the holding, setup, and production charges across the whole horizon. For every period, charge the holding cost on that period's ending inventory, charge the fixed setup cost whenever that period is set up for production, and charge the per-unit production cost on what is produced that period. Set the total cost equal to the sum of all these amounts over every period.

The objective is to minimize the total cost over the whole horizon.

params.name: demand

params.name: bigM
params.doc: a per-period upper bound on how much can be produced in that period; used as the large constant that links production to the setup decision, in units

params.name: invcoef
params.doc: the per-period inventory holding cost coefficient applied to ending inventory; equal to the base holding cost in every period except the final period of the horizon, where it is half the base holding cost, in dollars per unit per period

vars.doc: the inventory held at the end of each period, in units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity produced in each period, in units

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the setup indicator for each period; equals 1 if production is set up in that period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole horizon, in dollars; a scalar

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, allow production in a period only when that period is set up, keeping production within that period's upper bound when set up and at zero otherwise. Second, conserve inventory across periods so that incoming stock from the prior period plus current production meets demand and leaves the ending inventory, with the initial on-hand stock standing in for the prior period at the start of the horizon. Finally, set the total cost equal to the sum over all periods of the holding charge on ending inventory, the fixed setup charge in set-up periods, and the per-unit production charge.
